description_long: This is a small, rustic barn constructed entirely from cylindrical wooden logs arranged vertically for the walls and diagonally for the roof. The roof is a classic gable style, with a steep pitch and symmetrical slopes meeting at a central ridge. The front facade features a large, open rectangular doorway with no visible door, and a single square window to the right of the entrance, both framed by the log structure. The logs are closely packed, giving the barn a robust and traditional appearance reminiscent of early American log buildings. The sides and rear of the barn are solid, with no additional openings or architectural features. The overall design is simple and functional, suitable for storage or shelter in a rural or peri-urban setting.
description: A small, rustic log barn with a steep gable roof, open doorway, and a single square window on the front. The structure is made entirely of vertically arranged wooden logs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open rectangular doorway and a single square window to the right, both framed by vertical logs.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a solid wall of closely packed vertical logs with no openings or features.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, but with only the window visible and no doorway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to the first side, with a solid wall of vertical logs and no openings.
attribute_door_style: single large open doorway with no visible door
attribute_window_arrangement: single square window on the front facade, positioned to the right of the doorway
